channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Caitlin Clark officially named WNBA Rookie of the Year after historic season
item.description: After an historic season in which she set numerous records, superstar Caitlin Clark has officially been named the WNBA's Rookie of the Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Augusta chairman confident Masters will go on as club focuses on community recovery from Helene
item.description: Masters chairman Fred Ridley says Augusta National sustained a lot of damage from Hurricane Helene, just like the rest of the area.
item.dc:creator: Doug Ferguson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colorado's Shedeur Sanders has awkward moment with ex-NFL star: 'You be on that weird s---'
item.description: Colorado Buffaloes star quarterback Shedeur Sanders recently had an awkward interaction with former NFL star Cam Newton, and it was caught on video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Simone Biles' post-Olympic tour is helping give men's gymnastics a post-Olympic boost
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Simone Biles simply wanted to mix it up when the gymnastics superstar invited some of the top American men to join her post-Olympic Tour. “Bringing the guys on board was designed to show what men’s gymnastics has to offer,” Biles said.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: PFF adds an in-game grading feature to its NFL analysis
item.description: Pro Football Focus changed how many fans followed the NFL by providing grades and advanced statistics to help quantify how every player performed on a play-by-play basis from high-profile quarterbacks like Patrick Mahomes to more anonymous guards and linebackers.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Golf legend John Daly reveals Florida home destroyed in Helene: 'The memories is what you miss'
item.description: PGA Tour icon John Daly revealed his home in Clearwater, Florida, was destroyed in Hurricane Helene last week. He said he was glad everyone was OK.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Steve Kerr opens up on championship-or-bust culture: ‘This is not a zero-sum game’
item.description: In sharing his opinion on Ringz Culture, the head coach at least partially revealed his thoughts on the Warriors' roster.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: Chiefs star's mother eyed as possible package thief at apartment complex after son's injury
item.description: The mother of Rashee Rice was caught on camera allegedly stealing a package of Chappell Roan records from a neighbor's front door, shortly after her son's injury.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Higashioka's homer starts rally as Padres beat Braves 5-4 to sweep NL Wild Card Series
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Kyle Higashioka's solo homer started a five-run rally against an ailing Max Fried with two outs in the second inning, and the San Diego Padres held on to sweep the Atlanta Braves with a 5-4 victory in Game 2 of their NL Wild Card Series on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fried's early exit the latest Braves injury in a trying season that ends with playoff sweep
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The Braves' best pitcher, NL Cy Young Award favorite Chris Sale, was back home in Atlanta with a back injury.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How two Central football players are working to get recruited
item.description: A pair of local high school football stars -- Central High School's Jaime Padilla and Antonio Reyes -- are doing everything they can, even traveling the country to show college football programs what they can do.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Mitchell hits tiebreaking HR in the 8th, Chourio goes deep twice and Brewers rally past Mets 5-3
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Jackson Chourio tied the game in the eighth with his second homer of the night and Garrett Mitchell delivered a two-run shot later in the inning to give the Milwaukee Brewers a 5-3 victory over the New York Mets on Wednesday that evened their NL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri coach Eli Drinkwitz revokes football staffer's access due to brother playing for upcoming opponent
item.description: As Missouri continues to prepare for an SEC showdown against Texas A&M, one member of the Tigers football staff has been locked out of practice sessions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Rose's former teammate Tony Perez reflects on final moments with late baseball icon
item.description: Former Cincinnati Reds first baseman Tony Perez and Pete Rose developed a close friendship during their time together playing for the same MLB franchise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Orioles get swept out of the playoffs in their only series for a second consecutive year
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Things went so poorly for the Baltimore Orioles in the playoffs that even when they had the bases loaded and a hit by pitch scores a run, Colton Cowser swung at a ball thrown right at him, missed and broke his left hand in the process.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What position is Jonathan Kuminga? Entering Year 4, answers still diverge
item.description: Jonathan Kuminga thinks he's a small forward. So does Draymond Green. But how Steve Kerr deploys him remains a question.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: Bobby Witt Jr. and the Royals go from 2 late 7-game losing streaks to the AL Division Series
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — After another September loss gave the Kansas City Royals a second seven-game slide in the final month of the season, Will Smith saw the upcoming off day as a chance to make his teammates laugh. “You guys are looking at this the wrong way,” Smith said.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASCAR season hits final 6 weeks under shadow of antitrust lawsuit filed by 2 of its teams
item.description: The final six weeks of the NASCAR season will play out under the shadow of a lawsuit filed by the 23XI team and Front Row Motorsports against the series.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Team president Jed Hoyer disappointed about 2024, but optimistic about Cubs' direction
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The 2024 MLB playoffs began Tuesday, and the Cubs were once again not among the six teams in the National League playoff field.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tom Brady to announce 49ers-Cardinals game Sunday
item.description: (KRON) -- Tom Brady will announce his first San Francisco 49ers game on Sunday, according to his page on Fox's website. The ex-player considered the Greatest Of All Time by many made the switch to the announcing booth this season.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: High School Boys Golf: Grand Junction shines in regionals, state tournaments set
item.description: This week, the 4A Region 4 tournament took place at Black Canyon Golf Course in Montrose. If we learned anything, it's that the Grand Junction Tigers are for real.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: From food to murder, Aaron Rodgers talks all things Berkeley ahead of College GameDay appearance
item.description: (KRON) -- The college football world will have its eyes on Berkeley this weekend as Cal hosts ESPN's famous College GameDay show for the first time before its game against No. 8 Miami on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Aggies face another tough challenge at #21 Boise State
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The Aggies have had two weeks to stew in that second half collapse at Temple when they got outscored in the second half 28-8 to fall to 1-3 on the season. "Obviously, our record is not what we want," said quarterback Spencer Petras on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Reports: Pete Rose, baseball's all-time hits leader, dead at 83
item.description: LAS VEGAS — Pete Rose, one of Major League Baseball's most polarizing athletes and the game's all-time hits leader, has died at the age of 83, according to TMZ. Rose's agent, Ryan Fiterman, confirmed the news to TMZ and said, "the family is asking for privacy at this time.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Favre tries to expand his defamation lawsuit against Mississippi auditor over welfare spending
item.description: Favre is not facing criminal charges, but he is among more than three dozen people or businesses the state is suing to try to recover misspent money through the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mets reach playoffs, beat Braves to cap comeback from 22-33 start
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Four months after dropping 11 games under .500, the New York Mets earned a playoff berth a day after the regular season was supposed to end.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, Fox 5

item.title: Dikembe Mutombo, a Hall of Fame player, dies at 58 from brain cancer
item.description: Mutombo, a Basketball Hall of Famer who was one of the best defensive players in NBA history, died Monday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The final day for the Oakland Athletics arrives ahead of next season's move away from the Bay
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — When manager Mark Kotsay removed his Oakland jersey for the final time Sunday, it was bound for the Baseball Hall of Fame. Brent Rooker? His final jersey with the name Oakland across the front will be preserved in a frame and displayed on his wall at home.
item.dc:creator: TIM BOOTH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Americans get biggest road win and capture the Presidents Cup for the 10th straight time
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — U.S. captain Jim Furyk raised the gold trophy before a team that dressed in red shirts that filled the scoreboard early with red scores. The Presidents Cup is one trophy the Americans own, for two decades and counting.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: College GameDay makes first-ever appearance at Berkeley
item.description: (KRON) -- Since taking its show on the road back in 1993, "College GameDay" has been a huge presence in the college football community by being known to travel across dozens of campuses across the country.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: White Sox look to break Tigers' home win streak
item.description: Chicago White Sox (39-121, fifth in the AL Central) vs. Detroit Tigers (86-74, second in the AL Central) Detroit; Saturday, 1:10 p.m. EDT PITCHING PROBABLES: White Sox: Sean Burke (0-0); Tigers: TBD BOTTOM LINE: The Detroit Tigers, on a five-game home winning streak, host the Chicago White Sox.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SJSU opponent forfeits volleyball match, lawsuit alleges player is transgender
item.description: (KRON) -- Boise State University's volleyball team forfeited a match against San Jose State, the school announced on Friday. The cancellation came after one San Jose State player said in a lawsuit that one of her teammates is transgender.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Jazz management excited about youth movement
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Back in April, Jazz CEO of Basketball Operations Danny Ainge had his sights on acquiring a big-name player. "Our objective is to find a player or two, and we're we're ready to roll," Ainge said five months ago. "We're ready to go big game hunting.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
